zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard II 'le Bon' duc de Normandie於0963年8月23日出生在法國努維勒-阿吉泰洛吉吉加洛時，他的父親Richard 'Sans Peur' de Normandie comte de Rouen29歲，母親Gunnor de Crépon comtesse de Rouen24歲。他於1000年與Judith de Bretagne duchesse consort de Normandie在法國諾門迪結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他於996年被列為reign。他去世於1026年8月28日在法國瑟-易費利額費艾坎普享年63歲，埋葬在法國豪-諾門迪瑟-馬利費艾坎普。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Richard II 'le Bon' duc de Normandie于0963年8月23日出生在法国努維勒-阿吉泰洛吉吉加洛时，他的父亲Richard 'Sans Peur' de Normandie comte de Rouen29岁，母亲Gunnor de Crépon comtesse de Rouen24岁。他于1000年与Judith de Bretagne duchesse consort de Normandie在法国諾門迪结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和4个女儿的父母。他于996年被列为reign。他去世于1026年8月28日在法国瑟-易費利額費艾坎普享年63岁，埋葬在法国豪-諾門迪瑟-馬利費艾坎普。

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. nameMeanings.name: II
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Violet  ,   Vivien  , or   Vivian .

variants.name: Vivien
meanings: Earlier generally taken as a variant of the boy's name Vivian , but now also rarely used for boys. This spelling was quite common in Old French. Its use as a girl's name in the English-speaking world was influenced by Tennyson's Merlin and Vivien ( 1859 ). This name, from Arthurian legend, may represent an altered form of a Celtic name (perhaps akin to the Irish Gaelic name Béibhinn ‘white lady’, pronounced ‘bee-veen’). The actress Vivien Leigh ( 1913–67 ) was christened Vivian.

variants.name: Violet
meanings: From the name of the flower (Old French violette, Late Latin violetta, a diminutive of viola). This was one of the earliest flower names to become popular in Britain, being used as early as 1700 and becoming well established in the 19th century, although it is now somewhat out of favour.

variants.name: Vivian
meanings: Originally a boy's name, from an Old French form of the Latin name Vivianus (probably a derivative of vivus ‘alive’), but now more frequent as a girl's name. The name was borne by a 5th-century bishop of Saintes in western France, remembered for protecting his people during the invasion of the Visigoths.

nameMeanings.name: 'le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

nameMeanings.name: Bon'
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Altered short form of Robert , a later development than the common medieval forms Hob, Dob, and Nob, all of which, unlike Bob, have given rise to English surnames.

en.lifeSummary: When Richard II 'le Bon' duc de Normandie was born on 23 August 0963, in Lot-et-Garonne, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, France, his father, Richard 'Sans Peur' de Normandie comte de Rouen, was 29 and his mother, Gunnor de Crépon comtesse de Rouen, was 24. He married Judith de Bretagne duchesse consort de Normandie in 1000, in Normandy, France. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 4 daughters. In 996, his occupation is listed as reign. He died on 28 August 1026, in Fécamp, Seine-Inférieure, France, at the age of 63, and was buried in Fécamp, Seine-Maritime, Upper Normandy, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard II 'le Bon' duc de Normandie est né le 23 août 0963, à Lot-et-Garonne, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, France, son père, Richard 'Sans Peur' de Normandie comte de Rouen, avait 29 ans et sa mère, Gunnor de Crépon comtesse de Rouen, avait 24 ans. Il a épousé Judith de Bretagne duchesse consort de Normandie en 1000, à Normandie, France. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 4 filles. En 996, sa profession de son indiquée est reign. Il est décédé le 28 août 1026, à Fécamp, Seine-Inférieure, France, à l’âge de 63 ans, et a été enterré à Fécamp, Seine-Maritime, Haute-Normandie, France.

de.lifeSummary: Als Richard II 'le Bon' duc de Normandie am 23. August 0963 in Lot-et-Garonne, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Richard 'Sans Peur' de Normandie comte de Rouen 29 und seine Mutter Gunnor de Crépon comtesse de Rouen 24. Er hat 1000 in Normandie, Frankreich Judith de Bretagne duchesse consort de Normandie geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 4 Töchter. 996 ist sein Beruf als reign aufgeführt. Er starb am 28. August 1026 im Alter von 63 Jahren in Fécamp, Seine-Inférieure, Frankreich und wurde in Fécamp, Seine-Maritime, Haute-Normandie, Frankreich begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Richard II 'le Bon' duc de Normandie è nato il il 23 agosto 0963 nel Lot-et-Garonne, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, Francia, il suo padre, Richard 'Sans Peur' de Normandie comte de Rouen, aveva 29 anni e il suo madre, Gunnor de Crépon comtesse de Rouen, aveva 24 anni. Lui sposato Judith de Bretagne duchesse consort de Normandie nel 1000, nel Normandie, Francia. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 4 figlie. Nel 996, la sua occupazione è indicata come reign. Morì il 28 agosto 1026, nel Fécamp, Seine-Inférieure, Francia, all'età di 63 anni, e fu sepolto a Fécamp, Seine-Maritime, Haute-Normandie, Francia.

ja.lifeSummary: Richard II 'le Bon' duc de Normandieが0963年8月23日にフランスノウベレ-アクイティアインロト-エト-ガーオンで生まれた時，父Richard 'Sans Peur' de Normandie comte de Rouenは29歳，母Gunnor de Crépon comtesse de Rouenは24歳であった。1000年にフランスノーマンディでJudith de Bretagne duchesse consort de Normandieと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。996年，職業はreignと記録されている。彼は1026年8月28日にフランスセアイネ-インフeリーウアフeカンップで63歳で死去し，フランスハウテ-ノーマンディセアイネ-マリティメフeカンップにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard II 'le Bon' duc de Normandie(이)가 0963년 8월 23일 프랑스 노우벨레-아키테인 로트-트-가니에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Richard 'Sans Peur' de Normandie comte de Rouen(은)는 29세였고, 그의 어머니 Gunnor de Crépon comtesse de Rouen(은)는 24세였다. 그는 1000년 프랑스 노먼디에서 Judith de Bretagne duchesse consort de Normandie(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 996년, 그의 직업은 reign이었다. 그는 1026년 8월 28일 프랑스 세이인-인프e리우르 프e캠프에서 63세의 나이로 사망했으며 프랑스 하테-노먼디 세이인-마이팀 프e캠프에 묻혔다.

mn.23 August 963–28 August 1026: 0963 оны Наймдугаар сарын 23–1026 оны Наймдугаар сарын 28

pt.23 August 963–28 August 1026: 23 de agosto de 0963–28 de agosto de 1026
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Richard II 'le Bon' duc de Normandie nasceu em 23 de agosto de 0963, em Lot-et-Garonne, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, França, seu pai, Richard 'Sans Peur' de Normandie comte de Rouen, tinha 29 anos e sua mãe, Gunnor de Crépon comtesse de Rouen, tinha 24 anos. Ele casou-se com Judith de Bretagne duchesse consort de Normandie em 1000, em Normandie, França. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 4 filhas. Em 996, sua profissão aparece como reign. Ele faleceu em 28 de agosto de 1026, em Fécamp, Seine-Inférieure, França, com 63 anos, e foi sepultado em Fécamp, Seine-Maritime, Haute-Normandie, França.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard II 'le Bon' duc de Normandie родился 23 августа 0963 г. в местности лот-ет-гаронн, ноувелле-аквитеин, Франция, его отцу по имени Richard 'Sans Peur' de Normandie comte de Rouen было 29 лет, а матери, которую звали Gunnor de Crépon comtesse de Rouen, 24. Он женился на женщине по имени Judith de Bretagne duchesse consort de Normandie в 1000 г. в местности норманди, Франция. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 4 дочери. В 996 году он работал по профессии reign. Он умер 28 августа 1026 г. в местности фeкамп, сине-инфeриеур, Франция в возрасте 63 лет и был похоронен в местности фeкамп, сине-маритим, хауте-норманди, Франция.

sm.from 0996 to 1026: mai le 0996 e o'o I le 1026

es.23 August 963–28 August 1026: 23 de agosto de 0963–28 de agosto de 1026
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Richard II 'le Bon' duc de Normandie nació el 23 de agosto de 0963, en Lot-et-Garonne, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, Francia, su papá, Richard 'Sans Peur' de Normandie comte de Rouen, tenía 29 años y su mamá, Gunnor de Crépon comtesse de Rouen, tenía 24 años. Se casó con Judith de Bretagne duchesse consort de Normandie en 1000, en Normandie, Francia. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 4 hijas. En 996, su ocupación registrada era reign. Murió el 28 de agosto de 1026, en Fécamp, Seine-Inférieure, Francia, a la edad de 63 años, y fue sepultado en Fécamp, Seine-Maritime, Haute-Normandie, Francia.
